name: Hydrogen Atom (H)
description: A single hydrogen atom. ¹H: proton + electron. Atomic mass 1.007825 Da. Mass = 1.007825 × 1.66054×10⁻²⁷ kg = 1.6736×10⁻²⁷ kg. Contents: 1 proton (uud quarks), 1 electron. Nuclear binding energy: 0 MeV (no neutrons, no nucleus — just a bare proton). QCD binding energy: ~929.3 MeV (the proton's own quark confinement). The hydrogen atom is the simplest bound system: the proton proves that 99.04% of nucleon mass is QCD binding. The electron's Higgs mass (0.511 MeV) is σ-invariant.
